name: Arepa Zone

notes: Arepa Zone's dc-metro row was reconciled to the live restaurant-linked Square catalogs for Mosaico, 14th Street, and Western Market. Those menus publish 47, 56, and 52 products respectively and deduplicate to 75 stable product IDs across 14 categories.

All 49 frozen rows were reconciled: 2 exact products, 15 normalized products, 4 multi-section variants, 25 stale matrix-era rows, and 3 names produced by concatenating adjacent PDF rows. The matched frozen rows represent 24 current products; 51 current products were absent.
Forty-seven current products are not sold at all three DC locations. The repaired variantGroup records each product's live location set instead of implying metro-wide availability.
The current owner Shopify collection is an express-catering catalog, so it was used for corroborating owner product text, not as a replacement for the live regular ordering menus.

The owner allergen page currently links a five-page, 71-row matrix created in July 2024. Visual inspection transcribed the graphical allergen dots and the guide's global statement that all listed products are made in a facility processing wheat, milk, and eggs.

Thirty-three current products have unambiguous formulation matches to matrix rows. Matrix dots are stored as fixed allergens, while the wheat/milk/egg facility statement is stored separately as non-redundant mayContain signals on 30 rows.

Viuda Arepa is contact-only: the matrix marks wheat while also labeling the row gluten-free, and the current owner catalog also labels it gluten-free. No fixed wheat or negative safety claim is published; the facility contact warning remains.

Forty-one newer, packaged, or otherwise unmatched current products remain unavailable. The restaurant-linked Square field that names celery on Cruzado is retained in evidence but is not relabeled as restaurant-issued or forced into the app's profile vocabulary.
The current owner allergen page warns that supplier information, substitutions, and shared preparation can change exposure, so matrix coverage is not represented as a guarantee.
The frozen PDF text parser recovered row labels but dropped the vector-dot coordinates, leaving almost every official matrix row with an empty fixed-allergen array and omitting the facility warning.
The frozen parser also collapsed duplicate names across matrix sections and concatenated adjacent rows, producing names such as Patacón Viudo Tres Leches and Perro Caraqueño Pepito Fondue.

The reviewed builder contracts the three live location counts, deduplicates by stable Square product ID, records location scope, matches only defensible current formulations to a visually reviewed matrix transcription, and fails closed when any source count changes.

Focused tests cover all 75 current products, all 49 frozen dispositions, representative matrix dots, fixed-versus-facility semantics, the Viuda conflict, linked-vendor authority, beverages ordering, generated repair invariants, target-only isolation, and idempotency.

sourceAttempts.outcome: HTTP 200. Captured the current owner allergen page, its shared-preparation and supplier/substitution warning, and its active link to the five-page guide.
HTTP 200. Captured and visually reviewed all five matrix pages, 71 row presentations, eight allergen columns, nutrition labels, and the wheat/milk/egg facility statement.
notes: The PDF was created July 12, 2024 but remains the guide linked from the current owner page.

sourceAttempts.outcome: HTTP 200. The restaurant-linked Per Diem/Square ordering app exposed five active locations and current product APIs; the three DC catalogs contained 47, 56, and 52 products.
HTTP 200. Captured 74 current owner Shopify products and used matching text to corroborate positive owner-named ingredients, current common-fryer language, and the Viuda gluten-free conflict.
notes: This is an express-catering catalog and was not used as the regular-menu boundary.

findings.summary: The frozen PDF parser extracted labels but lost the graphical allergen dots, causing nearly every matrix row to appear to have no fixed allergens.
findings.resolution: Visually transcribed and regression-tested all 71 matrix rows, then mapped only defensible current formulations.

findings.summary: Only 24 of 75 current DC products were represented by matched frozen rows; 51 current products were omitted, 25 matrix-era rows were stale, and three frozen names were concatenation artifacts.
findings.resolution: Published the deduplicated 75-product live DC union and removed every stale or concatenated row.

findings.summary: All 21 matched frozen rows omitted the guide's wheat/milk/egg facility-processing statement.
findings.resolution: Stored facility processing separately as mayContain signals without turning it into fixed ingredients.

findings.summary: The frozen dc-metro row did not preserve that 47 current products are available at fewer than all three DC locations.
findings.resolution: Preserved each product's live location set in variantGroup and in its evidence summary.

findings.summary: The guide marks wheat for Arepa Viuda while the same row and current owner catalog label the product gluten-free.
findings.resolution: Published no fixed claim for Viuda Arepa and retained only the explicit facility-contact warning.

repairs.summary: Rebuilt the current three-location DC catalog, restored visual matrix and facility semantics, preserved location scope, removed stale/concatenated rows, and left unmatched current products unavailable.
